title: 1.1: Creates the ChecklistCard component as a named export in client/landing/stepper/declarative-flow/internals/components/checklist-card/index.tsx
1.2: Exports ChecklistCardItem interface with an icon field of type React.ReactElement and a text field of type TranslateResult from checklist-card/index.tsx
1.3: Accepts title (TranslateResult) and items (ChecklistCardItem[]) as props in ChecklistCard
1.4: Uses Card and CardBody from @wordpress/components as the outer container of the extracted shared component
1.5: Renders each item's icon and text content within the extracted shared component
1.6: Updates Authorization in authorization.tsx to import and use the extracted shared component instead of the inline AuthorizationBenefits component
1.7: Creates a dedicated stylesheet co-located with the extracted shared component
1.8: Tightens the vertical spacing between elements in the authorization step's layout by reducing the gap value in the authorization stylesheet
1.9: Widens the primary action button in the authorization step by applying a width or min-width style
1.10: Enhances the visual prominence of the secondary 'Share credentials instead' navigation link by adding styling such as font weight, underline, or color
3.1: Removes the inline AuthorizationBenefits component and its 2 associated interfaces (AuthorizationBenefitsItem, AuthorizationBenefitsProps) from authorization.tsx
3.2: Removes all 4 of the benefits-specific CSS rules (.benefits-container, .benefits, .benefits-item, .benefits-item-icon) from the authorization step's stylesheet
4.1: Removes or alters any of the three existing benefit items (the loop, backup, and shield icon entries with their translated text) from the Authorization component
4.2: Modifies components or logic unrelated to the benefits list extraction and the authorization step layout (such as altering NextButton behavior, callback handlers, or other stepper steps)
